name: GR 64 - Monks' Path
description: Jog the 26.3-mile Caminho dos Monges (GR 64) in Portugal, a difficult route with 2122 feet of gain through historic landscapes.
region_intro: Jogging routes around São João De Tarouca traverse a landscape rich in historical paths and diverse natural features. The region is characterized by the Varosa Valley, which offers scenic riverside routes, and extensive vineyards. Runners encounter varied terrain, including natural trails, paved sections, and gravel paths, often winding through green and wooded areas. The area's topography includes gentle hills and more challenging ascents, providing a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around São João De Tarouca The most popular jogging route is GR 64 - Monks' Path, a 26.3 miles (42.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This challenging route follows the Varosa River, passing historical landmarks and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Praia Fluvial do Varosa loop from Tarouca, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This route offers views of the Varosa Valley and includes sections near the river beach. Local runners also love the Running loop from Tarouca, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through local green areas, often completed in about 35 minutes. Jogging around São João De Tarouca is defined by river valleys, historical paths, and vineyard landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to long-distance trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 15 runners have used komoot to explore São João De Tarouca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in São João De Tarouca?
A: The running routes in São João De Tarouca offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll encounter natural trails, paved sections, and gravel paths, often winding through green and wooded areas. The region's topography includes gentle hills and more challenging ascents, providing a varied experience for runners. For example, the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2185987495/gr-64-monks-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 64 - Monks' Path&lt;/a&gt; features loose ground, paved sections, natural trails, asphalt, and gravel.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for different fitness levels in São João De Tarouca?
A: Yes, São João De Tarouca offers a range of running routes catering to various fitness levels. While the region features challenging long-distance trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2185987495/gr-64-monks-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 64 - Monks' Path&lt;/a&gt;, there are also moderate options such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Fluvial do Varosa loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.4 km long. Shorter, easier loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19576644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt; (5.5 km) are also available for a quicker run.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see while running in São João De Tarouca?
A: São João De Tarouca's running routes are rich in history and natural beauty. Many trails, particularly the Monks' Path, follow the Varosa River, offering scenic riverside views. You can encounter ancient structures like the 12th-century &lt;strong&gt;Monastery of São João de Tarouca&lt;/strong&gt;, Romanesque bridges, and the unique fortified bridge in &lt;strong&gt;Ucanha village&lt;/strong&gt;. The region is also known for its picturesque vineyards, especially within the Távora-Varosa and Douro wine regions, providing beautiful cultivated landscapes to run through.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running or jogging options in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided for all routes, shorter and less challenging loops would be suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19576644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt;, at 5.5 km with moderate elevation, could be a good option for families looking for a pleasant outdoor activity. The recreational park in Tarouca with its green and wooded areas also offers a peaceful environment for shorter, leisurely runs or walks.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in São João De Tarouca?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular running routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Fluvial do Varosa loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.4 km circular path offering views of the Varosa Valley. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanário – Memória à Mãe loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.5 km long and also circular, providing a convenient way to explore the surroundings.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in São João De Tarouca?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, including river valleys and vineyards, are enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, with vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but routes along the Varosa River or through wooded areas may provide some shade. Winter runs are also possible, though some sections might be more challenging depending on weather conditions.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by a river beach or offer waterside views?
A: Absolutely. The Varosa Valley is a prominent feature of the region, and many running routes follow the Varosa River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Fluvial do Varosa loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes sections near the river beach at Mondim da Beira, offering tranquil waterside views and green areas perfect for a refreshing run.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails in São João De Tarouca?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, the presence of towns like Tarouca and villages along the routes suggests that parking facilities are generally accessible. For routes starting from Tarouca, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19576644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanário – Memória à Mãe loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt;, you can typically find parking within the town or nearby.

Q: What kind of experience do other runners have in São João De Tarouca?
A: The running routes in São João De Tarouca are well-regarded by the komoot community, with over 15 runners having explored the varied terrain. Users often praise the blend of historical paths, diverse natural features, and scenic riverside routes along the Varosa Valley. The presence of well-maintained trails and options for different ability levels contributes to a positive running experience.

Q: Are there any running routes that incorporate ancient Roman roads or historical paths?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32164291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrada Romana loop from Gouviães e Ucanha&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.3 km route that specifically incorporates an ancient Roman road, offering a unique historical dimension to your run. Additionally, the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2185987495/gr-64-monks-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 64 - Monks' Path&lt;/a&gt; itself is a journey through centuries of heritage, connecting historical sites.

Q: How long are the running routes in São João De Tarouca?
A: The running routes in São João De Tarouca vary significantly in length. You can find shorter loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19576644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt; at 5.5 km, which can be completed in about 35 minutes. For longer runs, there are moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32166517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia Fluvial do Varosa loop from Tarouca&lt;/a&gt; (11.4 km). The most extensive route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2185987495/gr-64-monks-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 64 - Monks' Path&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 42.4 km trail.
tips:
From the wooden viewpoint, the view is breathtaking: down below, the Varosa River tears through the rock, passing under the enigmatic Devil's Bridge, surrounded by terraces that climb the slopes. 

Here, the walkways are your allies, as they soften a trail that would otherwise be a pure technical challenge, full of loose rocks and tight bends. A treat for those who enjoy running with excitement and scenery in between.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Caminho dos Monges (GR 64)?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse range of surfaces, keeping your jog engaging. You'll encounter approximately 17.7 km of loose ground, 11.6 km of paved paths, 5.4 km of natural trails, 4.77 km of asphalt, and 1.86 km of gravel. This mix means you'll experience everything from well-trodden paths to more rugged, natural sections.

faqs.question: Is the Caminho dos Monges (GR 64) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and a total vertical gain of around 800 meters, this trail is generally not recommended for beginner joggers. It requires good physical conditioning and sturdy footwear due to its varied and sometimes challenging terrain. For those new to long-distance jogging, it might be better to tackle shorter sections or consider other routes first.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and historical points along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in history and natural beauty. You'll jog through the Varosa and Douro river valleys, passing vineyards, medieval bridges, and traditional villages. Key historical sites include the Monastery of S. João de Tarouca (Portugal's first Cistercian monastery), the fortified bridge and tower of Ucanha, the Monastery of Salzedas, and the Chapel of S. Pedro de Balsemão. Don't miss the stunning views from Above the Varosa River Gorge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the trail in comfortable conditions, the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months are generally ideal. During these periods, the weather is typically milder, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful, whether with blooming flora or vibrant fall colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Caminho dos Monges (GR 64) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, agricultural areas, or near livestock. Be mindful of the trail's length and difficulty, and ensure your dog is fit enough for the journey. Always carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Caminho dos Monges (GR 64)?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Caminho dos Monges (GR 64) itself, as it is a public long-distance trail. However, some individual historical sites or monasteries along the route might have their own entrance fees if you choose to visit them.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Caminho dos Monges (GR 64)?
faqs.answer: The trail connects the municipalities of Lamego and Tarouca. You can typically find parking in or near the town centers of Lamego or Tarouca, which serve as the main trailheads. Specific parking areas might be available near the start points, but it's recommended to check local information for the most convenient options depending on your chosen starting point.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Lamego and Tarouca are accessible by public transport, primarily buses, from larger cities in the region. This makes it possible to plan a one-way jog and use public transport for your return journey. It's advisable to check current bus schedules and routes in advance, especially if you plan to divide the trail into multiple days.

faqs.question: Is there a recommended direction to jog the Caminho dos Monges (GR 64)?
faqs.answer: Jogging the trail from Lamego to Tarouca is considered significantly more physically demanding due to the elevation profile. If you are looking for a slightly less strenuous experience, consider starting in Tarouca and heading towards Lamego, as this direction might involve more downhill sections overall. However, both directions offer unique challenges and rewards.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Caminho Português do Interior, Mtb Caminho Português do Interior, and the Camino Torres - 02 Caminho de Don Diego de Torres Villarroel, among others. This allows for potential connections or variations if you wish to explore beyond the GR 64.
